sections.heading: Adverbs and Adverbials
sections.content:
An adverb describes the action of a verb (how, where, when).
Adverbials modify verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and prepositional phrases.
Examples:
He frowned angrily
The eclipse will occur soon

sections.heading: Functions
sections.content:
Modifier of verb: Mary solved the problem quickly
John visited Musa at Kebbi
Modifier of adjective: Peter is very tall
James is far more handsome than Silas
Modifier of another adverb: Bunmi runs extremely fast
He works hard enough to pass his exams
Modifier of prepositional phrase: The girl slapped Tom right in the face
We are quite on time for the party 

The main function of adverbs and adverbial phrases is to modify verbs. They also modify adjectives, other adverbs and prepositional phrases.
